   based on event.pointerType, which is unreliable across browsers: */

function openLightbox( event ) {
  // Intended: skip focus transfer for pointer/touch interactions,
  // apply only for keyboard. But:
  // Chrome/Opera: event.pointerType === '' on keyboard (expected)
  // Firefox/Safari: event.pointerType === undefined on keyboard
  // This condition therefore evaluates differently per browser.
  if ( event.pointerType !== '' ) {
    // Pointer/touch path: focus is NOT moved to the dialog.
    // Focus stays on the page behind the dialog:
    //   - tabbing navigates page content behind the modal
    //   - dialog is not announced by screen readers
    //   - Escape key does not close the dialog
    return;
  }
  // Keyboard path (only reached in Chrome, not Firefox/Safari):
  dialog.querySelector( '.close-button' ).focus();
}

function closeLightbox() {
  // Focus is not returned to the trigger button.
  // Result: complete focus loss — next Tab press
  // restarts from the document root.
  dialog.close();
}
